# Azion Docs — build

> All pages of the build section. Root index: https://www.azion.com/en/llms.txt

- [AI Inference](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/ai-inference/): Azion AI Inference empowers you to build and deploy intelligent applications that process data close to where it is generated.
- [Azion AI Inference Models](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/ai-inference/reference/models/): AI Inference offers a diverse range of edge-optimized models for various AI domains, ensuring efficient deployment and performance.
- [BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/ai-inference/reference/models/baai-bge-reranker-v2-m3/): BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3 is a lightweight reranker model with strong multilingual capabilities.
- [GPT-OSS 20B](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/ai-inference/reference/models/gpt-oss-20b/): GPT-OSS 20B is a large language model with 20 billion parameters, designed for text generation, conversation, and various natural language processing tasks.
- [InternVL3](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/ai-inference/reference/models/internvl3/): InternVL3 is an advanced multimodal large language model with capabilities to encompass tool usage, GUI agents, industrial image analysis, 3D vision perception, and more.
- [Mistral 3 Small (24B AWQ)](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/ai-inference/reference/models/mistral-3-small/): Mistral 3 Small provides a range of capabilities, including text generation, image analysis, embeddings, and more.
- [Nanonets-OCR-s](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/ai-inference/reference/models/nanonets-ocr-s/): Nanonets-OCR-s is an OCR model that converts document images to structured Markdown, preserving layout (headings, lists, tables) and basic tags. The output is easy to parse and feed into LLM pipelines.
- [Qwen2.5 VL AWQ 3B](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/ai-inference/reference/models/qwen-2-5-vl-3b/): Qwen2.5 VL AWQ 3B is a vision-language model that supports 3 bilion parameters and offers advanced capabilities such as visual analysis, agentic reasoning, long video comprehension, visual localization, and structured output generation.
- [Qwen2.5 VL AWQ 7B](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/ai-inference/reference/models/qwen-2-5-vl-7b/): Qwen2.5 VL AWQ 7B is a vision-language model that supports 7 billion parameters, offering advanced capabilities such as visual analysis, agentic reasoning, long video comprehension, visual localization, and structured output generation.
- [Qwen3 30B A3B Instruct 2507 FP8](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/ai-inference/reference/models/qwen3-30ba3b/): Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507-FP8 is an instruction-tuned 30B-parameter FP8 causal language model for long-context (256K) text generation and reasoning, supporting chat/QA, summarization, multilingual tasks, math/science problem solving, coding, and tool-augmented workflows.
- [Qwen3 Embedding 4b](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/ai-inference/reference/models/qwen3-embedding-4b/): Qwen3 Embedding 4B is a 4B-parameter multilingual embedding model (36 layers, 32K context) that outputs 2560‑dim vectors for text/code retrieval, classification, clustering, and bitext mining. It supports instruction-conditioned embeddings and is optimized for efficient, cross-lingual representation learning.
- [Applications](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/): Applications is a product that lets you build applications on Azion Global Network, reducing latency and throughput between origin servers and users.
- [How to configure Advanced Cache Key for Applications](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/advanced-cache-key/): Learn how you can improve the performance of your application and APIs by configuring content cache policies based on the Query String or Cookie of a page using Application Accelerator for Applications.
- [How to configure cache policies for Applications](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/cache-settings/): Learn how you can optimize processing times by defining the time-to-live (TTL) for cached content at the origin and at the browser, bypass origin cache settings, and forward cookies using Application Accelerator for Applications.
- [Verify Cache Indicators with ModHeader](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/check-page-cache-time/): You can debug requests to applications by creating headers that return cache indicators using the ModHeader extension for Google Chrome.
- [How to clone applications](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/clone-applications/): You can clone applications to replicate its settings for a new application.
- [How to configure main settings](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/configure-main-settings/): Customize your application settings, delivery protocols and modules.
- [Configure HTTP and HTTPS Ports](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/configure-ports/): Configure delivery and origin HTTP and HTTPS port combinations with Azion Application.
- [How to Fix CORS Policy Errors](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/cors-errors/): A Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) policy can block a request from a resource located in another domain different than the domain in use. Find out how to configure your application to fix this error.
- [How to create device groups](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/create-device-groups/): Learn how to create and manage Device Groups in Azion Console to tailor responses based on User-Agent, enhancing application responsiveness.
- [Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS)](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/cross-origin-resource-sharing-cors/): Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) is a mechanism for using HTTP headers to give access permission to a User Agent for specific resources that are on a different origin server to the document in use.
- [Crossdomain error with JW PLayer](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/crossdomain-error-in-jw-player/): When trying to run live streaming, the player displays the message “Cannot load M3U8 crossdomain access denied”.
- [How to customize an error response page](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/customizing-error-response-page/): Find out how to configure your Azion application to show custom error pages for your user based on HTTP error statuses.
- [How to debug rules created with Rules Engine](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/debug-rules/): You can use GraphQL, Data Stream, or Real-Time Events to debug any rule or rule set created using Rules Engine for Applications or Firewall.
- [How to delete an application](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/delete-applications/): You can delete your Azion application.
- [How to enforce HLS cache for live streaming delivery](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/enforce-hls-cache/): This guide covers the step-by-step to enforce HLS cache, managing chunks and playlists caching, and setting up Rules Engine rules.
- [How to enable gzip compression for Applications](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/gzip-compression/): With gzip compression, Azion ensures faster page rendering and downloads for your users.
- [Generate a Let's Encrypt Cert via API (HTTP-01)](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/how-to-generate-a-lets-encrypt-certificate-via-api/): Discover how you can generate a free TLS certificate issued by Let's Encrypt and automatically managed by Azion to secure your application via API.
- [Generate a Let's Encrypt Cert for Your Domain](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/how-to-generate-a-lets-encrypt-certificate/): Discover how you can generate a free TLS certificate issued by Let's Encrypt and automatically managed by Azion to secure your application.
- [Image Processor first steps](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/image-processor/): Optimize, resize, crop, and apply filters to your images automatically with the dynamic image processing capabilities of Azion Image Processor for Applications.
- [How to instantiate functions in your application](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/instantiate-functions/): Learn how to instantiate and activate serverless Functions within your Azion Applications, including setup via Azion Console and API.
- [Best practices for using Live Ingest](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/live-ingest-best-practices/): Learn the best practices to ensure high availability and resilience in your live streams with Azion's Live Ingest.
- [How to mitigate the HTTPOxy vulnerability](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/mitigating-a-vulnerability-httpoxy/): HTTPoxy is a web application vulnerability caused by malicious HTTP requests. Learn how to protect your application from attacks using Azion Rules Engine.
- [Configure Multiple Origins with Load Balancing](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/multiple-origins/): Explore how to configure Load Balancer for Applications on Azion, ensuring optimal traffic distribution and server efficiency with the Round Robin algorithm.
- [Send Client IP via a Dedicated HTTP Header](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/original-ip-header/): When a user makes a request to an application, the X-Forwarded-For header stores the client IP address, along with other IP addresses in the request route. Find out how you can isolate the clients IP into a dedicated header.
- [How to optimize image processing at the edge](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/process-images/): Manage image sizes, filters, watermarks, formats, and process images directly at the edge using a single URL query with Image Processor.
- [Create Request & Response Rules](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/rules-engine/): Rules Engine is an Applications capability that allows you to create no-code solutions that follow the business rules of your application.
- [How to set error pages](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/set-error-pages/): Set cache TTL for errors received from the origin and customize the error pages for your end-user.
- [How to stage an application through the hosts file](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/stage-applications-through-hosts-file/): By changing your device's name resolution in the hosts file under etc/hosts, you can test your application on the edge before pointing your domain to Azion.
- [Debug applications](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/troubleshooting/debug-applications/): Learn how to debug applications and functions using rules engine, Data Stream, and GraphQL API to enhance monitoring and error identification.
- [Understand metrics](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/troubleshooting/understand-metrics/): Explore how Azion Observe enhances monitoring of Applications and Functions with Real-Time Metrics, Events, and customized API data queries.
- [How to tune your cache settings](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/tune-cache-settings/): Boost your application's efficiency with strategic cache handling with custom cache TTL values, object segmentation methods, and large file optimization.
- [How to define a new origin for your application](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/work-with-origins/): Create an application with Azion by setting up an origin. Your origin can be an application, a web server, or storage.
- [Automate Behaviors with Rules Engine](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/guides/work-with-rules-engine/): Integrate rules in your application to set tasks for specific scenarios without changing your existing code.
- [Applications first steps](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/quickstart/): See the first steps for Applications.
- [Application Accelerator](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/application-accelerator/): Application Accelerator speeds up web applications and APIs through protocol optimizations and manages dynamic content requirements.
- [Cache Settings](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/cache-settings/): Configure cache TTL, large file segmentation, adaptive delivery, and cache rules for your application.
- [Cache](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/cache/): Cache speeds up content delivery by keeping your content cached at the edge and bringing your application closer to your users.
- [Tiered Cache](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/cache/tiered-cache/): Tiered Cache establishes an extra cache layer between the edge and your origin servers, effectively lowering latency and minimizing infrastructure expenses.
- [Certificate Manager](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/certificate-manager/): Manage TLS certificates for your HTTPS applications using Azion Certificate Manager.
- [Device Groups](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/device-groups/): Device Groups can help you manage content delivery and set specific behaviors according to the devices that access your application.
- [Domains](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/domains/): Azion domains keep your application content cached at the edge and can be customized to fit your needs.
- [Support for mTLS for Build](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/domains/mtls/): Understand how mTLS works at Azion.
- [Error Responses](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/error-responses/): Error Responses is an Applications capability that allows you to customize error pages for users based on the status code received from the origin.
- [Functions Instances](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/functions-instances/): Instantiate and execute the functions in your application directly at the edge.
- [Functions for Applications](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/functions/): Azion Functions allow you to create event-driven, serverless applications, at the edge of the network, closer to users.
- [Image Processor](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/image-processor/): Image Processor allows you to optimize, resize, crop, and apply filters to your images automatically. This provides a more dynamic and faster user experience in your web applications.
- [Applications Main Settings](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/main-settings/): Customize your application's modules and status.
- [Origins](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/origins/): Azion Origins allows you to customize the origins for your content, the Host header, and HMAC authentication credentials.
- [Real-Time Purge](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/real-time-purge/): Through Real-Time Purge you can instantly delete your content cache on Azion.
- [Rules Engine for Applications](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/rules-engine/): Rules Engine allows you to create conditional behaviors directly in Azion Console, automating your business logic and improving performance and throughput for your application.
- [Applications | v3](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/v3/): Applications is a product that lets you build applications on Azion Edge- Legacy version Network, reducing latency and throughput between origin servers and users.
- [Main Settings | v3](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/v3/main-settings/): Customize your application delivery with Azion's range of protocol options including HTTP, HTTPS, and HTTP/3 support for a better user experience and secure your connection with TLS ciphers.
- [WebSocket Proxy](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/applications/reference/websocket/): Boost the efficiency of your real-time applications with the WebSocket protocol for Azion Applications.
- [Connectors](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/connectors/): Connectors allows you to connect your origin to an Application. An origin represents the server from which Azion retrieves your content, such as an object storage service or a cloud server.
- [Load Balancer](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/connectors/reference/load-balancer/): Load Balancer allows you to balance traffic to your origin data centers or cloud providers to avoid network congestion and overloading your servers.
- [Origin Shield](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/connectors/reference/origin-shield/): Origin Shield is a security feature that provides a dynamic Network List, `Azion Origin Shield`, containing all IPv4 and IPv6 prefixes used by Azion's global network infrastructure.
- [How to Use the ALTCHA Function](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/altcha/): Azion’s ALTCHA Function is a serverless integration that protects your applications against bots and spam.
- [How to build an API with Functions and ChatGPT](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/api-builder/): Learn about the required process to create an API with Functions and ChatGPT and have the response shown on the Azion Preview Deployment.
- [How to build a browserless application with Functions](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/browserless-functions/): Learn how to implement a browserless application using Azion Functions to create efficient, secure, and scalable web solutions at the edge.
- [How to debug functions using GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/debugging-functions-graphql/): You can use the debugging process to identify errors in your functions.
- [Implement File Upload with Functions](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/file-upload-functions/): Learn how to create secure file upload functionality using Azion Functions and Object Storage to handle file uploads at the edge with optimal performance.
- [Create & Configure a Function on Firewall](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/firewall/): Functions run on the edge of the network, close to users. They can help you secure your applications through Firewall.
- [Functions first steps](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/first-steps/): Take the first steps to become an edge developer by developing functions.
- [Get Started With OpenNext](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/get-started/): Get started with OpenNext on Azion: create or adapt a Next.js app, configure caching and storage for ISR/SSG, develop locally with the Azion CLI, and deploy to production.
- [How to set up a paywall with Azion JWT solution](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/paywall-function-jwt/): JWT solution is a serverless function of Azion Web Platform for processing and validating JWT (JSON Web Token) tokens that can be used to create paywall solutions, allowing you to control access to content by subscription.
- [How to integrate Resend email service with Functions](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/resend-email-functions/): Learn how to integrate Resend email service with Azion Functions to send transactional emails directly from the edge with optimal performance and reliability.
- [Build a RESTful API with Functions & SQL](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/restful-tasks-api-functions/): Learn how to create a complete RESTful API for task management using Azion Functions and SQL Database, implementing CRUD operations at the edge for optimal performance.
- [How to run serverless functions on Azion](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/serverless-functions/): Functions is a service of Azion Web Platform that allows you to program and execute serverless functions, without the need to provision or manage servers.
- [How to handle Stripe webhooks with Functions](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/stripe-webhooks-functions/): Learn how to securely handle Stripe webhooks using Azion Functions to process payment events in real-time with optimal performance and reliability.
- [How to test an origin with Functions](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/test-origin-with-functions/): Use Functions in Firewall to mirror production traffic to a test origin. Validate new software with real user requests before deploying to production.
- [Troubleshooting an OpenNext Application](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/troubleshooting/): Learn how to troubleshoot OpenNext apps on Azion: run locally with Azion CLI, tail logs, use Real‑Time Events, Metadata & GraphQL APIs, fix size limits and environment variables.
- [Create a Function with WebAssembly](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/guides/webassembly-on-azion-platform/): Learn about the process required to create a function that uses a WebAssembly originated function.
- [Cache API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/cache/): The Cache API allows you to control reading and writing from Cache through Functions.
- [Azion and Next.js compatibility](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/nextjs/): Discover how various Next.js versions perform on the Azion Web Platform, highlighting compatibility, SSR, SSG, and essential middleware features.
- [Azion Runtime and Node.js API compatibility](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/): Explore the compatibility and integration of Node.js APIs and polyfills with the Azion Runtime, enhancing JavaScript execution in edge environments.
- [Node.js API compatibility - Async hooks](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/async-hooks/): This documentation explores how the Node.js async_hooks API can be effectively utilized within the Azion Runtime environment. The async_hooks module in Node.js enables developers to monitor the lifecycle of asynchronous operations.
- [Node.js API compatibility - Buffer](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/buffer/): Explore the integration and use of the Node.js Buffer API within the Azion Runtime. The Buffer API is crucial for handling binary data in Node.js, especially when dealing with file I/O or network operations where data is represented as streams.
- [Node.js API compatibility - Crypto](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/crypto/): Explore the integration and utilization of the Node.js Crypto API within the Azion Runtime. The Crypto module in Node.js provides cryptographic functionality that includes a set of wrappers for OpenSSL's hash, HMAC, cipher, decipher, sign, and verify functions.
- [Node.js API compatibility - Events](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/events/): Explore the integration and application of the Node.js Events API within the Azion Runtime. The Events module is a fundamental part of Node.js, providing the backbone for handling asynchronous events through the implementation of the EventEmitter class.
- [Node.js API compatibility - FS](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/fs/): Explore the integration and usage of the Node.js File System (FS) API within the Azion Runtime. The FS module provides a set of methods for interacting with the file system, allowing for reading, writing, and manipulating files and directories.
- [Node.js API compatibility - HTTP](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/http/): Explore the integration and application of the Node.js HTTP API within the Azion Runtime. The HTTP module in Node.js provides the necessary tools for building and managing HTTP servers and clients, facilitating the creation of web services and handling requests and responses.
- [Node.js API compatibility - Module](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/module/): This documentation examines the usage and compatibility of the Node.js Module System, which includes CommonJS and ES Modules, within the Azion Runtime. The module system is a core aspect of Node.js, allowing developers to organize code into reusable files and libraries.
- [Node.js API compatibility - OS](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/os/): This documentation explores the usage and compatibility of the Node.js OS module within the Azion Runtime. The OS module provides a variety of operating system-related utility methods and properties.
- [Node.js API compatibility - Path](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/path/): This documentation explores the integration and usage of the Node.js Path module within the Azion Runtime. The Path module provides utilities for working with file and directory paths.
- [Node.js API compatibility - Process](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/process/): This documentation explores the usage and integration of the Node.js Process module within the Azion Runtime. The Process module is an essential part of Node.js, enabling interaction with the current Node.js process, providing information, and controlling its execution.
- [Node.js API compatibility - Stream](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/stream/): This documentation explores the integration and use of the Node.js Stream module within the Azion Runtime. The Stream module is a fundamental part of Node.js, providing an abstract interface for handling streaming data.
- [Node.js Compatibility - String Decoder](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/string-decoder/): Explore the integration and utility of the Node.js String Decoder module within the Azion Runtime. The String Decoder module provides methods for decoding buffer data into strings, ensuring that multi-byte characters are properly handled without stream fragmentation issues.
- [Node.js API compatibility - Timers](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/timers/): Explore the integration and function of the Node.js Timers module within the Azion Runtime. The Timers module provides the ability to schedule code execution after a set period or at regular intervals.
- [Node.js API compatibility - Url](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/url/): Explore the integration and usage of the Node.js URL module within the Azion Runtime. The URL module provides utilities to parse and format URLs, manage query strings, and ensure correct URL manipulation.
- [Node.js API compatibility - Utils](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/utils/): Explore the integration and usage of the Node.js Util module within the Azion Runtime. The Util module provides a set of utility functions for various data processing tasks, including formatting strings, debugging, and extending objects.
- [Node.js API compatibility - VM](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/vm/): Explore the integration and application of the Node.js VM module within the Azion Runtime. The VM module provides utilities to run JavaScript code in a virtualized environment, offering a level of isolation from the main application.
- [Node.js API compatibility - Zlib](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/compatibility/node/zlib/): Explore the integration and functionality of the Node.js Zlib module within the Azion Runtime. The Zlib module provides compression and decompression utilities using the Gzip and Deflate/Inflate algorithms.
- [Debugging](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/debugging/): Explore debugging in Functions for enhanced control and observability, including setup guides for log management and Data Stream on the Azion platform.
- [Frameworks compatibility](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/frameworks-compatibility/): Explore how Azion Bundler integrates with JavaScript frameworks like Next.js, React, Vue on Azion Runtime, enhancing web development and performance.
- [Frameworks Compatibility | Angular](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/frameworks-compatibility/angular/): Explore how Azion Bundler integrates with Angular, a comprehensive platform for building web applications that provides a complete solution with built-in tools, TypeScript support, and powerful features for modern web development.
- [Frameworks Compatibility | Astro](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/frameworks-compatibility/astro/): Explore how Azion Bundler integrates with Astro, a modern static site builder that delivers lightning-fast performance with its content-focused approach and zero JavaScript by default philosophy.
- [Frameworks Compatibility | Gatsby](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/frameworks-compatibility/gatsby/): Explore how Azion Bundler integrates with Gatsby, a static site generator that compiles your React components into static HTML.
- [Frameworks Compatibility | Next.js](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/frameworks-compatibility/nextjs/): Explore how Azion Bundler integrates with Next.js, a flexible React framework for building high-performance full-stack web applications.
- [Framework Compatibility | React](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/frameworks-compatibility/react/): Explore how Azion Bundler integrates with React, a JavaScript library that uses components for user interface (UI) elements.
- [Environment Variables](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/environment-variables/): Explore how Environment Variables enhance security, flexibility, and compliance on the Azion platform, and their integration with Functions.
- [JavaScript Examples - A/B Testing](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/javascript-examples/ab-testing/): Configure an A/B test by regulating the type of response based on cookies.
- [JavaScript Examples - Adding a response header](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/javascript-examples/adding-response-header/): Explore how geo-location data in Edge Computing enables dynamic web application responses using Functions and JavaScript.
- [JavaScript Examples - Extract Cookie Value](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/javascript-examples/cookie-value/): Learn how to extract specific cookie values with JavaScript using functions for efficient A/B testing and HTTP header manipulation.
- [JavaScript Examples - Deny a request based on Geoip](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/javascript-examples/deny-request/): Learn how to manage geographic restrictions using JavaScript to access geoip country codes and control content accessibility through a dynamic firewall.
- [JavaScript Examples - Functions on Firewall](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/javascript-examples/general-firewall-example/): Explore JavaScript-based Functions for robust firewall interactions, featuring event handling, HTTP header adjustments, and custom responses.
- [JavaScript Examples - Hello World](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/javascript-examples/hello-world/): Return a Hello World string.
- [JavaScript Examples - Redirect All Requests](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/javascript-examples/redirect-requests/): Learn how to handle URL redirects using JavaScript with Functions, ideal for site maintenance or downtime strategies.
- [JavaScript Examples - Respond with Another Site](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/javascript-examples/respond-site/): Explore how to implement temporary redirects using JavaScript with the fetch API on the Azion platform, enhancing web development and response strategies.
- [JavaScript Examples - Rest APIs](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/javascript-examples/rest-apis/): Explore how to build HTTP requests like GET, POST, DELETE, PUT, and PATCH using the JavaScript fetch API, with practical examples and JSON data handling.
- [JavaScript Examples - Return HTML](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/javascript-examples/return-html/): Learn how to generate and deliver HTML pages real-time using JavaScript with Azion Functions, perfecting serverless web development.
- [JavaScript Examples - Return JSON](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/javascript-examples/return-json/): Learn how to use Functions to return JSON for APIs or middleware, featuring effective serverless computing with JavaScript.
- [JavaScript Examples - Using Args](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/javascript-examples/using-args/): Explore how to use arguments with event handling for web development, including async functions and JSON parameters.
- [Functions ChatGPT integration](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-api/ai-integration/): Explore how Functions integrate with ChatGPT APIs for enhanced coding productivity, featuring code generation, refactoring, and seamless API key integration.
- [Functions Code Editor](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-api/code-editor/): Explore the Functions Code Editor on Azion, featuring VS Code’s Monaco editor, ChatGPT integration, and tools like debugging and IntelliSense for efficient edge development.
- [Azion Preview Deployment](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-api/preview-deployment/): Explore how Azion's Preview Deployment enables real-time testing of Functions with features like Preview Provider, API builders, and ChatGPT integration.
- [JavaScript Runtime APIs - AddEventListener](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/add-eventlistener/): The addEventListener function defines the trigger for the execution of JavaScript code, receiving the request data.
- [Azion Runtime Globals Web APIs](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/azion-runtime-globals/): Explore supported JavaScript data types and objects for Azion Runtime, including WebAssembly, Crypto API, and event handling, enhancing Edge computing development.
- [CryptoKey](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/crypto-key/): The CryptoKey interface represents a cryptographic key obtained from one of the SubleCrypto methods.
- [Crypto](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/crypto/): The Crypto interface allows access to a cryptographically strong random number generator and to cryptographic primitives.
- [JavaScript Runtime APIs - Encoding](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/encoding/): Encoding
- [EventTarget](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/event-target/): The EventTarget interface is used by objects that can receive events and have listeners set up to handle those events.
- [JavaScript Runtime APIs - FetchEvent](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/fetch-event/): Fetch Event
- [JavaScript Runtime APIs - Fetch API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/fetch/): Fetch
- [Intl](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/intl/): The ECMAScript Internationalization API uses the Intl object as a namespace for providing language-sensitive features such as string comparison, number formatting, and date and time formatting. The Intl object contains a range of constructors and functions that can be used for this purpose, including functionality that is common to both internationalization constructors and other language-sensitive functions.
- [ReadableStreamBYOBReader](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/readable-byob-reader/): Learn about ReadableStreamBYOBReader.
- [ReadableStreamDefaultReader](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/readable-default-reader/): Learn about ReadableStreamDefaultReader.
- [ReadableStream](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/readable-stream/): The ReadableStream interface is a feature of the Streams API that allows the reading of streams of byte data. The Fetch API provides a specific instance of a ReadableStream that can be accessed through the \"body\" property of a Response object.
- [JavaScript Runtime APIs - Request](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/request/): Request
- [JavaScript Runtime APIs - Reponse](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/response/): The Response interface represents an HTTP response and integrates the Fetch API.
- [WritableStreamDefaultWriter](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/stream-default-writer/): Explore the WritableStreamDefaultWriter from the Streams API, including methods like write, close, and abort, and learn how it manages data streams effectively.
- [Structured clone](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/structured-clone/): Explore the Structured Clone Algorithm in JavaScript, essential for duplicating objects and handling exceptions like DataCloneError, useful in various APIs.
- [Crypto Subtle](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/subtle-crypto/): The SubtleCrypto interface provides a number of low-level cryptographic functions.
- [TransformStream](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/transform-stream/): Learn about TransformStream.
- [URLPattern](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/url-pattern/): Learn about URLPattern.
- [JavaScript Runtime APIs - Web Standards](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/web-standards/): Explore essential JavaScript standards and APIs, including Math and JSON objects, with security insights and additional developer resources.
- [JavaScript Runtime APIs - WebAssembly](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/webassembly/): Explore the WebAssembly interface, functions like compile and instantiate, syntax, and examples for effective JavaScript integration.
- [WritableStream](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/writable-stream/): Explore the WritableStream interface of the Streams API, crucial for handling data streaming with backpressure, properties, and methods in web development.
- [Environment Variables interface](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime/api-reference/environment-variables/): The Environment Variables interface can be used inside Functions to retrieve environment variables. You can use these variables to interact with backend systems like databases, private APIs, or any authenticated service.
- [Handlers](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime/api-reference/handlers/): Azion Functions supports JavaScript handlers. Handler functions let you control how your edge logic manages incoming requests, applies security rules, and interacts with the runtime environment. You can define handler functions by using either the ES Modules pattern or the Service Worker syntax.
- [Metadata API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime/api-reference/metadata/): Explore how Functions and Firewall utilize metadata for enhanced security, including GeoIP, TLS, and remote client details to manage and secure access.
- [Network List interface](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/functions/runtime/api-reference/network-list/): The Azion.networkList.contains interface can be used by functions on Firewall to match an IP address against a specific network list. If the IP address informed is within the network list, it returns true and the desired implementation logic can go on based on that information.
- [How to resolve Node.js APIs through polyfills](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/guides/use-polyfills/): Learn how to configure and use Node.js APIs through polyfills.
- [Functions - JavaScript Examples](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/javascript-examples/): Examples of syntax patterns you can use in Functions - JavaScript.
- [Web APIs](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/javascript/): Check out the documentation for the JavaScript Runtime Environment on Azion's Edge Computing platform.
- [KV Store API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/kv-store/): The KV Store API provides methods to store, retrieve, and delete key-value pairs from within your Functions.
- [Delete key-value pairs](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/kv-store/delete/): To remove a key-value pair from KV Store, call the delete() method on your KV instance.
- [Read key-value pairs](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/kv-store/read/): To get the value for a given key, call the get() method on your KV instance.
- [Write key-value pairs](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/kv-store/write/): To create a new key-value pair, or to update the value for a particular key, call the put() method on your KV instance.
- [Azion Runtime](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/overview/): The Azion Runtime is a set of tools for developing applications at the Edge. The Azion Runtime allows you to write JavaScript functions built on Web standards. Functions help you to implement the business logic and create serverless applications, at the edge of the network, closer to users.
- [SQL Database API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/sql-database/): The SQL Database API provides an interface for interacting with a database from Functions running on the Azion Web Platform.
- [Object Storage API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/storage/): The Functions Storage API is an interface that allows access to the Azion Object Storage. Through this interface, it's possible to read and write data in the storage and its buckets.
- [WebSocket API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/functions/runtime-apis/websocket/): WebSocket Proxy allows your Application to act as a full WebSocket server or client and proxy bidirectional traffic to backend services.
- [Build](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/overview/): Build and run applications on Azion's Edge Computing network, ensuring low-latency processing closer to end users.
- [Workloads](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/workloads/): Workloads is an Azion Console tool that centralizes communication protocol settings, such as certificates, domains, ports and protocols.
- [How to configure a domain](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/workloads/guides/configure-a-domain/): Use Azion Domains to enable browser access to your app, allowing user traffic and metrics tracking.
- [How to create a digital certificate](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/workloads/guides/create-a-digital-certificate/): Digital certificates are essential for online security, providing authentication and encryption for your data.
- [How to Create an Azion Custom Domain](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/workloads/guides/create-azion-custom-domain/): Learn how to create a Custom Azion Domain using Workloads.
- [How to migrate nameservers to Azion](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/workloads/guides/migrate-ns-to-azion/): Migrate your authoritative nameservers to Azion for improved control and reliability of your DNS settings at the edge.
- [How to point a domain to Azion](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/build/workloads/guides/point-domain-to-azion/): Point your domain record to your Azion domain, ensuring a seamless user experience with your application.
